definitions.text: To, toward, or from the far side of (something that lies between two points of interest).
examples: Fortunately, there was a bridge across the river.

definitions.text: On the opposite side of (something that lies between two points of interest).
examples: That store is across the street.

definitions.text: across from: on the opposite side, relative to something that lies between, from (a point of interest).
From one side to the other.
examples: she helped the blind man across; the river is half a mile across
If we sail off at noon, when will we be across?
He leaned across for a book.

definitions.text: A word that runs horizontally in the completed puzzle grid or its associated clue.
examples: I solved all of the acrosses, but then got stuck on 3 down.
